mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0 percent, labor market slack triggers margin compression and earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds 4.0 percent, or the signal loses predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and aggregate retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive 60-day return in more than 60 percent of triggered quarters, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression framework.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the industrial production index during late-cycle expansions signal supply chain bottlenecks and inventory drawdowns, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 5-day returns in more than 50 percent of late-cycle contraction months, or the predictive accuracy drops below baseline during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPIAUCSL index surpasses 315, persistent inflation expectations force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing equity discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 315, or the threshold effect vanishes in a walk-forward validation over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and a simultaneously rising unemployment rate indicates labor force participation shocks or measurement noise, creating earnings uncertainty that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction signal yields positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of months, or the cross-term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in housing starts reflect strong household balance sheets and construction sector activity, forecasting higher corporate capital expenditures and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with HOUST increases.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid rebounds in industrial production following a macroeconomic shock signal aggressive fiscal stimulus and supply chain normalization, triggering immediate positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 5-day returns in more than 50 percent of post-shock recovery months, or the predictive accuracy drops below baseline during recovery phases.
